MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Deactivate the inoperative nose wheel steering channel (AMM DDG 32-51-01).

ListItem.Para: Identify the inoperative nose wheel steering channel on the LNDG GEAR INDN AND STRNG maintenance page. The inoperative channel (SYS 1 or SYS 2) will be indicated by a blank display or STBY displayed in the CHANNEL STATUS field.
For System 1 inoperative, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open circuit breaker NOSE WHEEL STRG REU-PRI and lock with an INOP tag.
For System 2 inoperative, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open circuit breaker NOSE WHEEL STRG REU-SEC and lock with an INOP tag.

OperationNote.Para: The NOSE WHEEL STEERING CHAN status message will display when one Nose Wheel Steering Channel is inoperative.

The NOSE WHEEL STEERING advisory and status messages may be displayed when both engines are off, the center hydraulic system is unpressurized and either the NOSE WHEEL STRG REU-PRI or NOSE WHEEL STRG REU-SEC circuit breaker is open. The message will clear when the center hydraulic system is pressurized. If the message stays shown, dispatch is not permitted.
